name: Kit's Chocolate Mousse
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the chocolate over hot water, stirring until smooth. Let it cool until only warm.
2. Whip the cream with a whisk or electric mixer until it forms medium-stiff peaks. Do not overbeat; stop beating once soft peaks form to maintain texture.
3. Separate 4 of the eggs into two bowls: whites in one, yolks in the other. In the bowl with yolks, beat until thick and lemon-colored (about 5 minutes).
4. Whisk the egg whites in a clean copper or stainless steel bowl until they begin to stiffen. Gradually add confectioner's sugar while whisking until firm peaks form.
5. Add the cooled melted chocolate and some whipped cream to the yolk mixture, stirring until smooth. Incorporate the remaining cream gradually, then fold in the egg whites until just combined.
6. Pour the mousse into small serving dishes or one larger dessert dish. Chill for at least 4 hours before serving; chilling longer (overnight) enhances flavor. The mousse can be frozen up to 2 weeks and thawed overnight in the refrigerator before serving.
7. Variation: Use a Brownie Cookie Crust from the Brownie Cookies recipe, fill it with the mousse, chill for at least 4 hours, then pipe whipped cream on top.
